bootstrap.author: verdigris
bootstrap.title: The paint on my stairwell railing is worn through at exactly the spots where everyone's hand lands
bootstrap.body: Four floors, same smooth patches at every turn. Decades of neighbours all reaching for the same three inches of railing without ever agreeing to.

comments.author: GlassOnion88
comments.body: when this happens on grass it's called a desire path. I love that railings get them too

replies.author: verdigris
replies.body: a desire path for hands. I'm going to be thinking about that all week

comments.author: chinook_wind
comments.body: the old bank building downtown has a brass door plate worn from gold to pink in one thumb-sized spot. same thing, fancier metal

comments.author: zamboni_dreams
comments.body: the boards at our rink have this where every player taps their stick coming off a shift. one spot, worn to bare wood

bootstrap.author: chinook_wind
bootstrap.title: It hailed on my side of the street today while the other side stayed in full sunshine
bootstrap.body: Calgary summer doing its thing. My neighbour across the road kept watering his lawn through the whole thing, out of what I can only assume was spite.

comments.author: RAWfiles
comments.body: did you get a photo? sun through falling hail is one of those shots you get one chance at

replies.author: chinook_wind
replies.body: of course not, I was busy standing in it being amazed. the eternal trade-off

comments.author: verdigris
comments.body: so the middle of the street was the border between two different days

comments.author: portage_and_main
comments.body: prairie weather refuses to commit and honestly I respect it. we once barbecued through a June snow flurry

bootstrap.author: GlassOnion88
bootstrap.title: Paused a movie last night and the background newspaper is the same prop newspaper that shows up in dozens of other shows
bootstrap.body: Same layout, same photos — they just swap the headline. One prop house has been printing it for decades, so it quietly circulates through productions everywhere. Once you've seen it you can't unsee it.

comments.author: cassette_futurism
comments.body: prop houses are full of this. the Wilhelm scream is the audio version — same recorded yell turning up in hundreds of films since the 50s

comments.author: verdigris
comments.body: a fictional newspaper with a longer filmography than most working actors

replies.author: GlassOnion88
replies.body: and it has range. background of a sitcom one week, serious crime drama the next

comments.author: zamboni_dreams
comments.body: this is the kind of thing I'd never have noticed and will now notice every single time. thanks, I think

bootstrap.author: zamboni_dreams
bootstrap.title: The white of hockey rink ice is paint — it gets sprayed on in layers between thin coats of ice
bootstrap.body:
Watched the crew rebuild the sheet at our arena this week. Fresh ice is clear grey concrete colour until the white goes on, then the lines and logos, then more ice sealed over top.

The whole rink is basically a painting under glass.

comments.author: penaltyboxphil
comments.body: played on outdoor rinks my whole childhood and not once questioned why indoor ice was whiter. huh

comments.author: chinook_wind
comments.body: 'a painting under glass' is such a nice way to put it. does it need touch-ups through the season?

replies.author: zamboni_dreams
replies.body: deep gouges get patched, and you can spot the repairs if you know where to look. like art restoration, but with a hose

comments.author: GlassOnion88
comments.body: this is prop-newspaper energy but for sports. the set dressing was under our skates all along

drip.author: RAWfiles
drip.title: Caught a double rainbow tonight and the second one's colours run in reverse
drip.body: Never noticed this before: the faint outer bow goes violet on the outside and red on the inside — a mirror image of the main one. That's just how the second reflection works, apparently. Shot it, obviously.

comments.author: northof60
comments.body: the dark strip between the two bows has a name too — Alexander's band. once you see it you look for it every time

replies.author: RAWfiles
replies.body: just checked my shot and the dark band is right there. today the sky got more interesting

comments.author: verdigris
comments.body: reversed colours on the second one feels like the sky doing a mirror check

comments.author: chinook_wind
comments.body: we get doubles after hailstorms constantly and I have never once clocked the reversal. going to be insufferable about this now

drip.author: verdigris
drip.title: Every clock on the thrift store wall was stopped at a different time, except two that agreed
drip.body: Eleven frozen moments and one small conspiracy.

comments.author: GlassOnion88
comments.body: 'one small conspiracy' — the two that agree are obviously in on something

comments.author: quietkettle
comments.body: there's something lovely about a wall of clocks that all gave up at different moments. like a guest book of afternoons

replies.author: verdigris
replies.body: a guest book of afternoons. that's exactly what it was

comments.author: zamboni_dreams
comments.body: and twice a day the whole wall is briefly right, with those two being right together

drip.author: chinook_wind
drip.title: My neighbour's sprinkler makes a perfect little rainbow at 7:40 every evening and I've started scheduling around it
drip.body: Right angle, right sun, lasts about four minutes. The dog and I now take our walk at rainbow o'clock.

comments.author: RAWfiles
comments.body: golden hour plus backlit water is free magic. cherish your municipal light show

comments.author: verdigris
comments.body: 'rainbow o'clock' should be an official unit of summer time

replies.author: chinook_wind
replies.body: it drifts a couple minutes later each week as the sun angle shifts, so it's a unit that slowly runs away. seasonal daylight tax

comments.author: GlassOnion88
comments.body: the dog has no idea the walk is scheduled around atmospheric optics, and that somehow makes it better

drip.author: GlassOnion88
drip.title: Same background extra crosses the shot twice in one continuous scene — once with a hat, once without
drip.body:
Old movie, single take, no cut. Man walks left to right wearing a hat. Eight seconds later, same man, same coat, no hat, walks right to left.

Either a continuity gaffe or the most subtle time-loop film ever made.

comments.author: zamboni_dreams
comments.body: he's clearly walking a loop behind the set and someone stole his hat between laps. there's a whole story back there

comments.author: RAWfiles
comments.body: extras get recycled through backgrounds constantly — the hat swap is someone in wardrobe assuming nobody would ever freeze-frame. they did not plan for you

replies.author: GlassOnion88
replies.body: decades of pause buttons have betrayed so many hard-working background loops

comments.author: verdigris
comments.body: I choose to believe time loop. the hat is the only witness

late_comments.author: RAWfiles
late_comments.body: there's a photo series hiding in this. worn spots where hands go — bannisters, push plates, pew backs

late_comments.author: chinook_wind
late_comments.body: spotted the exact newspaper in a rerun last night and pointed at the tv like a maniac. thanks for this

late_comments.author: GlassOnion88
late_comments.body: half a street in a hailstorm is a split-screen effect happening for free

late_comments.author: verdigris
late_comments.body: 'a painting under glass' has been rattling around my head since I read this. galleries wish they had a zamboni
